We Build

Reliability.

PRJ. is an independent practice for backend logic, web applications, and the quiet pipelines that turn raw rows into something your team can decide from.

Practice

Explore PRJ.

001 / Backend

SQL & Stored Procedures

Backend logic institutions actually rely on. Schema design, query optimisation, batch jobs that finish on time. Audit-friendly, traceable, written to be readable two years from now.

Learn More →
002 / Frontend

Web Applications

Hand-built sites and small applications for businesses that have outgrown templated builders. Lean. Self-hostable. Designed to last past the next five frameworks.

Learn More →
003 / Data

Pipelines & Reports

From messy CSVs and live databases into something a dashboard reads without complaint. ETL where it belongs — out of sight. The story before the spreadsheet.

Learn More →

A working practice — not flash, not scale-at-all-costs. The work I take on tends to be specific, scoped, and quietly important. The kind that runs every morning whether anyone is watching or not.

Reach

Working with teams across timezones.

Based in Bangalore. Available across IST, GMT, and EST. Async-first by default — synchronous when the work demands it.

Trust

What partners say

Working partnerships with institutions and businesses that need their systems to simply work — not to impress.

The examination allocation procedure replaced a manual process that took our staff three days. Now it runs in four minutes, every term, with the audit trail we needed for accreditation.
Dr. M. — Examination Branch Higher Education Institution, Bangalore
The grade-wise reporting was silently producing wrong numbers for two years. Within a week of the rewrite, we had verified results going to the registrar without manual checks.
Mrs. P. — Academic Systems Higher Education Institution, Bangalore
Hand-built site, no monthly subscription, complete ownership. Two years on, it has not gone down once. Exactly what was promised.
Mr. S. — Owner Local Business, Banashankari
Notes

Insights & Perspectives

Working notes from a practice — on systems, data, and the quiet craft of writing software that lasts.

May 2026 · Field Note

On NULL Propagation: A Quiet Source of Wrong Answers

How a single NULL in a JOIN can silently produce incomplete reports for years — and the discipline that catches it before production.

Read more →
April 2026 · Essay

The Audit Trail Is the Product

Why the most important feature of a stored procedure isn't what it does today — it's what you can prove about what it did six months ago.

Read more →
March 2026 · Field Note

Owning the Asset: Why Hand-Built Sites Still Win

For local businesses, a custom-built site without a monthly subscription is closer to property than to software. A small case for the long form.

Read more →
